papers.title: Sparks of Artificial General Intelligence: Early Experiments with GPT-4
papers.summary: Wide-ranging qualitative study showing GPT-4 exhibits cross-domain reasoning, planning, and tool-use that go materially beyond prior LLMs — and a sober assessment of where it still fails.

insurance_business_case.headline: Frames the executive narrative for AI investment: which insurance tasks are now feasible vs. which still need humans-in-the-loop.
points: Use the paper's failure modes (math drift, hallucination, planning depth) to draw the line between AI-automated and AI-assisted insurance workflows.
Justifies investment in agent frameworks for compound tasks (multi-step underwriting) vs. single-call APIs for simple ones (FAQ).
Provides language for board-level AI strategy / risk papers.

technical_details.pattern: Capability-vs-risk matrix per insurance workflow, mapped to LLM strength/weakness.
points: Build a capability scorecard per LOB (Life, Health, Annuity, Group) × workflow stage.
Score against the paper's known failure axes (arithmetic, multi-hop, recency, hallucination).
Pair with NIST AI RMF for the risk side; use to prioritize AI roadmap funding.

papers.title: AutoGen: Enabling Next-Gen LLM Applications via Multi-Agent Conversation
papers.summary: Framework where specialized LLM agents (planner, coder, critic, executor) converse to solve tasks — the basis of Microsoft's agent stack.

insurance_business_case.headline: Production pattern for breaking complex insurance processes into specialist AI roles that mirror today's org chart.
points: New-business: 'Intake agent' + 'Underwriter agent' + 'Pricing agent' + 'Compliance agent' converse to bind a policy.
Claims: 'Triage agent' + 'Medical agent' + 'Fraud agent' + 'Settlement agent' jointly recommend a disposition.
Distribution: 'Lead-qualification agent' + 'Needs-analysis agent' + 'Illustration agent' for assisted producer journeys.

technical_details.pattern: Role-based multi-agent system on AutoGen / Semantic Kernel, with a critic agent enforcing compliance.
points: Define agents in code with explicit system prompts, allowed tools, and stop conditions.
Conversation orchestrator persists every message to immutable storage for audit.
Critic agent runs against the Constitutional-AI-style policy file (see Anthropic tab).
Cost & latency budgets per case; SLA-trip routes to humans automatically.

papers.title: Phi-3: A Highly Capable Language Model Locally on Your Phone
papers.summary: 3.8B-parameter open model trained on heavily curated 'textbook-quality' data that matches GPT-3.5-level reasoning at a fraction of the cost — runs offline on a phone.

insurance_business_case.headline: Enables AI assistants where the cloud LLM is impossible: agent-laptop offline mode, hospital kiosks, regulated-data zones.
points: Field-agent app that captures, validates, and pre-fills applications offline in rural / low-connectivity markets (Asia, Africa LATAM).
On-prem deployments in jurisdictions with strict data-residency (e.g., certain group health books).
Cost-driven downshift: route ~70% of low-complexity intents (FAQ, status checks) to Phi-class models, GPT-4-class only for hard cases.

technical_details.pattern: Tiered LLM routing: small on-device / on-prem model for the easy majority, frontier model for the long tail.
points: Router classifies intent + difficulty (a tiny BERT or rules) and selects the model tier.
On-device: ONNX Runtime + DirectML / Core ML; quantize to INT4 for laptop / phone footprint.
Continuous distillation: log frontier-model outputs on hard cases, retrain Phi periodically.
Privacy posture: PII never leaves device unless escalated; explicit consent on escalation.

papers.title: Orca 2: Teaching Small Language Models How to Reason
papers.summary: Demonstrates that small models can be taught when to use Chain-of-Thought vs direct answer vs step-by-step explanation, narrowing the gap with much larger models on reasoning benchmarks.

insurance_business_case.headline: Affordable accuracy gains for the high-volume, decision-rich workflows where running GPT-4 on every call is uneconomic.
points: Premium calculation explainers in the call centre — small model gives consistent, traceable rationales.
Adjudication co-pilot for short-term disability claims (10s of thousands of decisions/month).
Auto-letter generation with mandatory CoT rationale for compliance sampling.

technical_details.pattern: Reasoning-distilled small LM fine-tuned on expert teacher traces.
points: Build teacher corpus: GPT-4 CoT explanations on 100k anonymized historical adjudications.
Supervised fine-tune Phi/Orca-class student; reinforce on agreement with teacher final answer.
Serve on dedicated GPU pool with vLLM / TensorRT-LLM for throughput.
Drift monitor: weekly agreement with teacher on rolling sample → trigger refresh.

papers.title: VASA-1: Lifelike Audio-Driven Talking Faces Generated in Real Time
papers.summary: Generates a photo-realistic talking head from a single image and audio clip, in real time. Microsoft published with explicit deepfake-risk framing.

insurance_business_case.headline: Two-sided implication: enables next-gen customer / training experiences AND raises a material new fraud vector that risk functions must address now.
points: Opportunity: multilingual personalized policy explainers and producer training avatars at scale.
Threat: synthetic-identity onboarding, deepfake video-claim submissions, deepfake CEO fraud against finance.
Compliance: drives liveness-detection, voice-print, and provenance (C2PA) controls into the identity stack.

technical_details.pattern: Generative AI threat model + defensive controls embedded in onboarding and claims intake.
points: Liveness: passive (texture, micro-expression) + active (random prompt) checks on every video submission.
C2PA content-credentials verification on submitted media; reject unsigned where feasible.
Voice-biometric enrolment on policy issue; verify on phone-channel claims and beneficiary changes.
Red-team program: regularly attack your own intake with deepfake samples; track false-accept rate.
